user_message: The CSA escalated Blue Yonder Airlines to me -- can you identify the blockers?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 13:15 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 10:35 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-03-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:12:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:18:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:22:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:30:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:35:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:39:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-J trap (Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-J failure mode (Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-J -- Talking-point generic where customer-specific is warranted
